channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Hacker News] Can the stockmarket swallow Anthropic, SpaceX and OpenAI?
item.description: Can the stockmarket swallow Anthropic, SpaceX and OpenAI?

item.title: [Hacker News] OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
item.description: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS

item.title: [Simon Willison] Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked
item.description: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked

item.title: [Hacker News] Alphabet announces $80B equity capital raise to expand AI infra and compute
item.description: Alphabet announces $80B equity capital raise to expand AI infra and compute

item.title: [Hacker News] GitHub and the crime against software
item.description: GitHub and the crime against software

item.title: [OpenAI] Our views on AI policy and political advocacy
item.description: Our views on AI policy and political advocacy

item.title: [Hacker News] AI Agent Guidelines for CS336 at Stanford
item.description: AI Agent Guidelines for CS336 at Stanford

item.title: [Hacker News] DuckDuckGo makes its 'no-AI' search engine easier to access as its traffic booms
item.description: DuckDuckGo makes its 'no-AI' search engine easier to access as its traffic booms

item.title: [Hacker News] Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over AI risks
item.description: Florida sues OpenAI and Sam Altman over AI risks

item.title: [Hacker News] Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC
item.description: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC

item.title: [Anthropic] Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC
item.description: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC

item.title: [Latent Space] Why Video Agent models are next — Ethan He, xAI Grok Imagine Lead
item.description: Why Video Agent models are next — Ethan He, xAI Grok Imagine Lead

item.title: [Hacker News] The Pirate Bay Remains Resilient, 20 Years After the Raid
item.description: The Pirate Bay Remains Resilient, 20 Years After the Raid

item.title: [Hacker News] CS336: Language Modeling from Scratch
item.description: CS336: Language Modeling from Scratch

item.title: [Hacker News] No Raise, No Promotion: 1 in 4 White-Collar Workers Are Stalling Out
item.description: No Raise, No Promotion: 1 in 4 White-Collar Workers Are Stalling Out

item.title: [Interconnects] Open and closed models are on different exponentials
item.description: Open and closed models are on different exponentials

item.title: [Hacker News] When AI Crosses the Line: The Matplotlib Incident
item.description: When AI Crosses the Line: The Matplotlib Incident

item.title: [OpenAI] Building the infrastructure for the Intelligence Age in Michigan
item.description: Building the infrastructure for the Intelligence Age in Michigan

item.title: [GitHub Trending] pbakaus/impeccable — The design language that makes your AI harness better at design.
item.description: Impeccable is a design language tool aimed at enhancing AI design capabilities.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] TauricResearch/TradingAgents — TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework
item.description: GitHub project TradingAgents provides a multi-agent LLM framework for financial trading.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] can1357/oh-my-pi — ⌥ AI Coding agent for the terminal — hash-anchored edits, optimized tool harness, LSP, Python, browser, subagents, and more
item.description: AI coding agent for terminal using hash-anchored edits with LSP, Python, and subagent support.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] stefan-jansen/machine-learning-for-trading — Code for Machine Learning for Algorithmic Trading, 2nd edition.
item.description: Code for Machine Learning for Algorithmic Trading, 2nd edition released on GitHub.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/dmtrKovalenko — The fastest and the most accurate file search toolkit for AI agents, Neovim, Rust, C, and NodeJS
item.description: A file search toolkit for AI agents, Neovim, Rust, C, and Node.js is trending on GitHub.

item.title: [OpenAI] OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
item.description: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] QASM-Eval: A Dataset to Train and Evaluate LLMs on OpenQASM-3 Beyond Quantum Circuits
item.description: QASM-Eval provides 4,000 training and 100 test tasks to train and evaluate LLMs on OpenQASM-3 quantum programming, emphasizing hardware-facing features.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Gait2Hip-60: A Unified Deep Learning Benchmark for Predicting Hip Muscle Forces and Joint Moments from Multi-Cadence Gait Kinematics
item.description: Gait2Hip-60 benchmark compares deep learning models for predicting hip muscle forces from gait kinematics, with Transformer achieving best performance on healthy subjects and patients.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Unicorn: Scaling High-Dimensional Time Series Forecasting via Universal Correlation Modeling
item.description: Unicorn decouples time series correlation from channel identity using latent prototypes, enabling scalable pretraining and transfer across heterogeneous datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When LLMs Learn to Be Consistently Wrong: A Multi-Model Study of Linear Representations of Synthetic Deception
item.description: Linear probes detect synthetic deception in transformer models with 0.99+ AUC in early layers, supporting linear representation hypothesis.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LLMs Without Deep Neural Networks: New Architecture, Benefits and Case Study
item.description: RBF network approach for LLMs achieves global optimization in closed form with single iteration, claiming higher explainability and accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Functional MRI Time Series Generation via Wavelet-Based Image Transform and Spectral Flow Matching for Brain Disorder Identification
item.description: Researchers propose DSFM, a generative framework using wavelet and cosine transforms for realistic fMRI data synthesis to improve brain disorder identification.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Novel Evaluation Metric for Unsupervised Learning in AIS-Based Maritime Anomaly Detection: MADQI
item.description: New evaluation metric MADQI enables unsupervised anomaly detection in maritime AIS data without requiring labeled training datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] NumLeak: Public Numeric Benchmarks as Latent Labels in Foundation Models
item.description: NumLeak shows frontier LLMs memorize public numeric benchmarks during pretraining rather than learning generalizable skills.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LongDS-Bench: On the Failure of Long-Horizon Agentic Data Analysis
item.description: LongDS benchmark reveals maintaining analytical state—not interaction budget—is key bottleneck in long-horizon data analysis, with best models reaching 48.45% accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Calibrated Preference Learning: The Case of Label Ranking
item.description: Research formalizes calibration for probabilistic label ranking and finds existing models are often poorly calibrated.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Bounded Behavioral Indistinguishability for Black-Box LLM Distillation
item.description: Framework for evaluating LLM distillation finds semantic similarity improvements don't guarantee behavioral indistinguishability from teacher models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] VeriGate: Verifier-Gated Step-Level Supervision for GRPO
item.description: VeriGate extends GRPO by combining verifier rewards with process supervision and token-level credit, improving reasoning model accuracy by 12-20%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Unified Framework for Gradient Aggregation in Multi-Objective Optimization
item.description: Unified framework for gradient aggregation in multi-objective optimization establishes convergence to Pareto stationarity and introduces capped MGDA algorithm.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DisjunctiveNet: Neural Symbolic Learning via Differentiable Convexified Optimization Layers
item.description: DisjunctiveNet enables exact hard constraint satisfaction in neural networks through differentiable convex relaxations of disjunctive rules.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Scalable Constrained Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning via State Augmentation and Consensus for Separable Dynamics
item.description: Distributed MARL method using consensus on dual variables achieves linearly-scalable constraint satisfaction for multi-agent coordination with separable dynamics.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] idSCD: Identifying Training Datasets through Semantic Correlation Descriptors
item.description: Method identifies training datasets by analyzing semantic correlation structures learned by models, using semantic fingerprinting instead of behavioral signals.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Can Subgraph Explanations Be Weaponized to Steal Graph Neural Networks?
item.description: Model extraction attacks can steal graph neural networks through their explainability interfaces by observing only class labels and explanation masks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Universal Multiclass Transductive Online Learning
item.description: Paper characterizes learnability of transductive online classification with unbounded labels using LCLL trees, identifying two optimal error rate patterns.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Discovering a Zeta Map Algorithm on Dyck Paths via Mechanistic Interpretability
item.description: Researchers extract a provably correct Dyck path algorithm from a small transformer using mechanistic interpretability, matching the classical zeta map.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Graph-Conditioned Mixture of Graph Neural Network Experts for Traffic Forecasting
item.description: Spatio-temporal traffic forecasting framework assigns personalized GNN expert combinations per node via graph-conditioned routing, improving accuracy while training only 17K lightweight parameters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MAAT: Multi-phase Adapter-Aware Targeted Unlearning
item.description: Researchers introduce 5WBENCH benchmark and MAAT method to better evaluate and improve machine unlearning for causal knowledge.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Revisiting Padded Transformer Expressivity: Which Architectural Choices Matter and Which Don't
item.description: Study proves padded transformers match circuit complexity classes, with precision and depth—not width—determining their computational expressivity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Representation Collapse in Sequential Post-Training of Large Language Models
item.description: Sequential post-training of LLMs compresses representations into low-rank spaces, impairing plasticity and generalization; interventions like replay and regularization mitigate this.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Measuring, Localizing, and Ablating Alignment Signatures in LLMs
item.description: Post-training alignment induces detectable AI-like stylistic patterns in LLMs; researchers develop PASTA, a method to measure and remove these signatures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Long-Term Effects of Data Selection in LLM Fine-Tuning
item.description: LLM data selection strategies optimized for short-term performance can harm future adaptability and increase model forgetting.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DisasterLex: An Expert Concept-to-Schema Knowledge Graph for Geospatial Reasoning in Disaster Analytics
item.description: DisasterLex, a knowledge-graph framework, improves text-to-SQL queries for disaster management databases by 1.4x-2.75x over existing methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SubsurfaceGen: Procedural Generation of Field-Scale Earth Models and Seismic Data
item.description: SubsurfaceGen procedurally generates realistic field-scale 3D seismic training data for machine learning-based full waveform inversion.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Early Prediction of Future Behavioral Strategy from Process Traces
item.description: Model predicts individual behavioral strategy across tasks by extracting person-level patterns from partial process traces.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Destruction is a General Strategy to Learn Generation; Diffusion's Strength is to Take it Seriously; Exploration is the Future
item.description: Diffusion models use information destruction as a flexible training approach, outperforming hand-crafted withholding techniques especially in data-scarce settings.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Supervised Training Rapidly Degrades Early Visual Cortex Alignment Across Biologically Plausible Learning Rules
item.description: Training reduces neural network alignment with early visual cortex within one epoch, with backpropagation degrading alignment more than local learning rules.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Protocol for evaluating ChatGPT in biomedical association generation and verification using a RAG-enabled, cross-model majority voting workflow
item.description: Protocol evaluates ChatGPT's biomedical association generation using RAG-enabled majority voting to verify associations and detect hallucinations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Exploring Autonomous Agentic Data Engineering for Model Specialization
item.description: LLM agents autonomously curate and iteratively optimize domain-specific training data, achieving 57% improvement in model specialization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Domain Adaptation and Reasoning Frameworks in Language Models: A Controlled Experiment with Historical Cosmology
item.description: Domain adaptation shifts language model explanatory frameworks toward premodern styles without substantially changing underlying cosmological stance, per a controlled historical cosmology study.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Cross-Lingual Steering for Figurative Language Generation
item.description: Activation steering reveals reusable cross-lingual signals for controlling figurative language generation in multilingual large language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Can LLM Teams Play What? Where? When?
item.description: LLM teams outperform individual models on a quiz game requiring collective reasoning, gaining up to 20 percentage points through shared communication strategies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Knowledge Graph-Enhanced Zero-Shot Topic Classification: A Multi-Strategy Comparative Study
item.description: Study evaluates zero-shot topic classification using knowledge graph augmentation, finding keyword enhancement effective and graph benefits limited to smaller language models.
